title: Latent account type treated as an observed screening label
prompt: A platform sets one screening threshold from a noisy account-risk score before admitting users. Benign or malicious type remains latent. A proposed policy applies different thresholds after directly observing each account's true type. Diagnose the hidden-information error and frame the implementable score-based rule.

model_specification.mathematical_objective: maximize expected platform value from benign admissions net of malicious harm and rejection loss
state_variables: latent benign or malicious account type
constraints: one common threshold for all accounts
model_specification.mechanism: The noisy score changes posterior risk, so the threshold trades false rejection of benign accounts against admission of malicious accounts.
model_specification.uncertainty: latent account type and risk-score realization

fidelity_requirements: True account type is not observed at admission
The policy conditions on the score through one common threshold
prohibited_inferences: The platform directly observes benign or malicious type
The threshold can vary by the account's true type

evidence_needs.claim: Conditional score distributions differ by latent type
